evidence_summary: Transition the visual language from a dark, storm-ravaged Kansas aesthetic to a highly saturated, lush Oz landscape. The environment must feel surreal and vibrant through extreme color density in flora and fruit-bearing trees.
Dorothy wakes up following the cyclone to find herself not in a storm-ravaged Kansas, but in a lush, colorful landscape filled with flowers and fruit.
Dorothy waking up in a state of confusion and physical disarray.
Dorothy looking out at the vibrant world in awe.
Dorothy awakens and processes the sensory shift from storm to paradise.
